questions.question: Which of the following describes the induced fit model of enzyme action?

options.A: The active site is flexible and changes its shape to tightly bind the substrate
options.B: The active site is perfectly complementary to the substrate before binding
options.C: The enzyme is irreversibly inactivated by the substrate
options.D: The substrate changes its shape completely while the enzyme remains rigid

What is the primary mechanism by which proteins are absorbed in the small intestine?
What is the function of the brush border enzymes in the small intestine?

options.A: To break down proteins into amino acids
options.B: To break down carbohydrates into sugars
options.C: To break down fats into fatty acids

questions.question: What is the function of the pancreatic juice in digestion?

options.A: To break down proteins into amino acids
options.B: To break down carbohydrates into sugars
options.C: To break down fats into fatty acids

questions.question: What is the function of the ileum in the small intestine?

options.A: To absorb bile salts and vitamin B12

questions.question: What is the function of the cecum in the large intestine?

options.B: To break down cellulose into glucose
options.C: To house a diverse community of microorganisms
